risk_flags: EU storage at 42.48% (18th pctile, 14.3 pp below seasonal) — refill pace critical; any LNG disruption or warm summer will extend tight gas regime into autumn.
Strait of Hormuz closure (Feb 28) elevated crude and supports power/gas fuel-switch; shipping fragmentation adds 3–5 weeks to LNG delivery timelines, tightening European supply.
Clean Spark at 89th pctile (31.25 EUR/MWh) — gas in-the-money vs coal; AI demand-side legislation targets peak shaving but implementation lags weeks; near-term peak volatility remains elevated.
Chinese sanctions on Russian-linked firms risk delays to renewables components and grid infra; constrains deployment velocity and supports longer-term power curve inflation.

base.summary: Tight storage, firm TTF, power elevated; AI demand-side legislation delays; geopolitics contained.

downside.summary: Hormuz de-escalates; LNG flows accelerate; mild weather, renewables surge; demand-side flexibility gains traction.

upside.summary: Hormuz escalation or LNG delivery delays; storage refill disappoints; AI demand peak-shaving stalls.

themes: Tight EU gas via storage deficit; TTF premium into refill season
Power demand surge (AI, industry) compresses margins; peak-shaving policy nascent
Geopolitical risk premium: Hormuz closure, shipping fragmentation tightens LNG arb
Renewables 45th pctile; coal soft; carbon stable but tail-risk to supply-chain sanctions
top_drivers: Storage deficit (14.3 pp below seasonal, 18th pctile) locks in tight-gas regime into refill season; TTF +2.7% and 66th pctile reflect LNG supply anxiety.
Hormuz geopolitical risk and shipping fragmentation tighten LNG arb to Europe; crude elevation supports fuel-switch premium and power margin compression (Clean Spark 89th pctile).
AI/industrial electricity demand surge collides with nascent peak-shaving policy; DA volatility and thermal call remain extended until AI-driven demand flexibility deployed (weeks+ horizon).
top_takeaway: Storage 14.3 pp below seasonal; TTF +2.7% and power tracking 77th–87th percentile amid demand surge from AI/industry and Hormuz geopolitical risk.

watchlist.why: 42.48% full (18th pctile, 14.3 pp below seasonal) — injection slope and LNG flow timing set H2 gas and power risk through autumn.
Feb 28 closure triggered crude spike; continued disruption supports TTF via LNG arb tightening and power margin compression; monitor weekly for escalation/resolution.
Peak-shaving legislation aims to reduce marginal generation cost and grid strain; timeline slippage prolongs elevated DA/intraday volatility; watch for pilot programme dates.
Supply-chain bottlenecks in semiconductors and renewables components delay grid modernisation; structural upside to Cal+1 power curve; monitor EU Commission sanction lists weekly.

watchlist.metric: US-EU trade truce / Trump tariff threats
watchlist.why: Forced labour tariff negotiation outcome affects EU export costs and industrial electricity demand; tail-risk to power demand forecast and TTF via industrial feedstock demand.
